title: Shaktheeswaram Temple
tagline: Shaktheeswaram Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Shaktheeswaram Temple is a Hindu temple located in Kottayam, India.
overview.description: Shaktheeswaram Temple is a Hindu temple located in Kottayam, India. The main deity of the temple is Goddess Adi Parashakti. Adi Parashakti is the female aspect of the divine venerated as the Supreme Being in Shaktism. The temple is also a center where Traditional Astrology, Tantric Upasana, Sanathana Dharma and the most auspicious pooja in Hinduism called Sri Chakra Pooja is practiced by Head Priest and Tantracharya Sri Kasi Viswanathan.

topPlaces.name: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Cherthala in Alleppey district in the Indian state of Kerala. Believed to be constructed in the 7th century by Azhvanchery Thamprakkal.

topPlaces.name: Vayalar
topPlaces.description: Vayalar (Malayalam: [ʋɐjɐlaːr]) is a village in Cherthala taluk, Alapuzha district, Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Pattanakkad
topPlaces.description: Pattanakkad is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. This is a panchayat which come under the Cherthala Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple is one of the Bhadrakali temples in Cherthala, Alappuzha district, Kerala. It is about 1 kilometer west of the Arthungal bypass of NH 47 at Cherthala.

image.alt: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple

topPlaces.name: Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Cherthala is a suburb and satellite town of Kochi City located in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India, along National Highway 66. As per the 2011 Indian census, Cherthala has a population of 45,821 people, and a population density of 2,727/km2 (7,060/sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Kadakkarappally
topPlaces.description: Kadakkarappally is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is a panchayat which comes under the Cherthala Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala is a famous Hindu temple located at Cherthala. Iratti and Thadi are the famous vazhipadu, Cherthala pooram is the second famous pooram in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Vettakkal
topPlaces.description: Vettakkal is a coastal village in Cherthala taluk of Alappuzha district in Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shaktheeswaram Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shaktheeswaram Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shaktheeswaram Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (50 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (29 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shaktheeswaram Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shaktheeswaram Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shaktheeswaram Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Shaktheeswaram Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shaktheeswaram Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple, Vayalar, Pattanakkad, Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple, Cherthala.
